questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (4,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,3);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below] {$q$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left] {$p$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right] {$s$};\coordinate (va) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(B)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(C)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(A)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which side is the hypotenuse?

options.why: Chose a shorter side (a leg) that meets the right angle instead of the side opposite it.
Assumed the bottom horizontal side is always the hypotenuse.

questions.solution_text: The hypotenuse is $s$, the side opposite the right angle.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (J) at (1,1.732);\coordinate (L) at (-2,0);\coordinate (K) at (2,0);\draw (J)--(L) node[midway,above left] {$j$};\draw (J)--(K) node[midway,above right] {$k$};\draw (L)--(K) node[midway,below] {$m$};\coordinate (va) at ($(J)!0.3cm!(L)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(J)!0.3cm!(K)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(J)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The right angle is at the top vertex. Which side is the hypotenuse?

options.why: Picked a slanted leg forming the right angle at the top, not the side opposite it.
Picked the other slanted leg forming the right angle rather than the opposite side.

questions.solution_text: The hypotenuse is $m$, the base opposite the right angle at the top vertex.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (4.5,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,2);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below] {$12$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left] {$5$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right] {$13$};\coordinate (va) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(B)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(C)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(A)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which side length is the hypotenuse?

options.why: Chose the longer leg instead of the side opposite the right angle.
Chose the shortest side rather than the longest side, the hypotenuse.

questions.solution_text: $13$, the side opposite the right angle and the longest side.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.9]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (W) at (0,2.5);\coordinate (F) at (2,0);\draw (A)--(W) node[midway,left] {wall};\draw (A)--(F) node[midway,below] {ground};\draw (W)--(F) node[midway,above right] {ladder};\coordinate (va) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(F)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(W)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(A)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A ladder leans against a wall, making a right angle between the wall and the ground. Which distance is the hypotenuse?

options.text: The horizontal distance from the wall to the foot of the ladder
options.why: This is a shorter side along the ground, a leg that meets the right angle, not the side opposite it.

options.text: The vertical height the ladder reaches up the wall
options.why: This is the other leg, running up the wall to the right angle, not the side opposite it.

questions.solution_text: The ladder is the hypotenuse, the side opposite the right angle between the wall and the ground.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.8]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (2,-1);\coordinate (C) at (1,2);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below] {$u$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left] {$v$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right] {$w$};\coordinate (va) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(B)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(A)!0.3cm!(C)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(A)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
This right-angled triangle is rotated so the right angle points down. Which side is the hypotenuse?

options.why: Picked a leg meeting the right angle because the triangle is rotated, not the opposite side.
Picked the other leg at the right angle instead of the side opposite it.

questions.solution_text: The hypotenuse is $w$, the side opposite the right angle at $A$.
